name: HillaryClinton.com
subject: re: Matt Lauer
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Hillary for America, a grassroots campaign of 2
body:
We need to keep Trump in check if the media won't | This is a longer email than I usually send you, Derek, but I wanted to share this important column from Jonathan Chait I read last night: [
](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=3&ms=MjY0NTg1NTES1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODgxMzUyNzk2S0&mt=1&rt=0) Chait is far from the only media observer discussing the extent to which Lauer fell flat in trying to interview the two candidates for president. But Chait actually discusses what the failures mean, and in doing so, he keys in on something important.

"The average undecided voter is getting snippets of news from television personalities like Lauer," he writes, "who are failing to convey the fact that the election pits a normal politician with normal political failings against an ignorant, bigoted, pathologically dishonest authoritarian."

If you're on this email list, you've come to know a lot about Donald Trump -- his racist and divisive policies, his complete lack of qualifications for the presidency, and his visceral allergy to the facts.

But most voters aren't like us, Derek. Most people are picking up on politics when it finds them on Facebook, on the radio in the car, or when they flip through a magazine in line at the grocery store.

Their information is filtered through the press. And right now, a lot of journalists are failing to hold Trump accountable and grading him on a curve, while forcing Hillary to meet an entirely different standard.

So instead of most voters hearing about how Trump is empowering a new generation of white supremacists, for instance, and having that news placed in a proper, terrifying context, they read stories of Hillary and Trump lumped together.

And that makes our jobs in this election all the more important. We have to do what the media won't do, Derek. We have to be on the air, online, and at people's front doors, talking to them honestly about the stakes of this race.

**And all of that takes resources. It takes you. I'm counting on you. Chip in right now for the first time, get a free sticker, and make sure we can hold Trump's feet to the fire:** [](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=7&ms=MjY0NTg1NTES1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODgxMzUyNzk2S0&mt=1&rt=0)
Thanks,

Christina

Christina Reynolds
Deputy Communications Director
Hillary for America

|
**Hillary needs you now more than ever -- chip in $1 and join the 2.3 million grassroots donors standing with her:**

| [](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=8&ms=MjY0NTg1NTES1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODgxMzUyNzk2S0&mt=1&rt=0)

---

**** | | Paid for by Hillary for America, a grassroots campaign of 2.3 million donors committed to electing Hillary Clinton (and keeping Donald Trump out of the White House).
---
Contributions or gifts to Hillary for America are not tax deductible.
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. This is your campaign, so if you have thoughts on anything at all, just [click here](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=15&ms=MjY0NTg1NTES1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODgxMzUyNzk2S0&mt=1&rt=0) to send us a message! If you'd like more information on key policies, visit [hillaryclinton.com/issues](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=1&ms=MjY0NTg1NTES1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODgxMzUyNzk2S0&mt=1&rt=0). Opening this email on your iPhone? [Click here](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=4&ms=MjY0NTg1NTES1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODgxMzUyNzk2S0&mt=1&rt=0) to get our Hillary 2016 mobile app now. Getting emails from Hillary for America is one of the best ways to stay in touch with this campaign, but if you really want to scale back, click [here](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=13&ms=MjY0NTg1NTES1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODgxMzUyNzk2S0&mt=1&rt=0) to receive less email and click [here](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=11&ms=MjY0NTg1NTES1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODgxMzUyNzk2S0&mt=1&rt=0) to unsubscribe. There are other ways to stay involved -- be sure to follow the campaign on [Facebook](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=MjY0NTg1NTES1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODgxMzUyNzk2S0&mt=1&rt=0), [Twitter](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=6&ms=MjY0NTg1NTES1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODgxMzUyNzk2S0&mt=1&rt=0), and [Instagram](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=10&ms=MjY0NTg1NTES1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODgxMzUyNzk2S0&mt=1&rt=0). Puedes encontrar más información sobre la campaña de Hillary en español [aquí](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=12&ms=MjY0NTg1NTES1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODgxMzUyNzk2S0&mt=1&rt=0). If you're still reading this, you must be a really dedicated Hillary supporter. [Yee-haw for you](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=9&ms=MjY0NTg1NTES1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODgxMzUyNzk2S0&mt=1&rt=0)! Thanks so much for doing everything you can to elect Hillary Clinton as our next president.
Hillary for America, P.O. Box 5256, New York, NY 10185
[Privacy Policy](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=5&ms=MjY0NTg1NTES1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODgxMzUyNzk2S0&mt=1&rt=0)

name: Bob =?UTF-8?Q?Fertik=2C=20Democrats=2Ecom?=
subject: These four unique landscapes need our help...NOW!
body:
[Sign: Tell President Obama to create national monuments in these four historic parts of America »](http://democrats.ontraport.com/c/s/EFw/6jSZf/6/A/r5cs/6FTash/shL2bX0Qxk)

|
---|---
| Petition to President Obama:

"_Thank you for creating the world 's largest marine protected area! I urge you to keep building on your conservation legacy by establishing national monuments in Utah's Bears Ears region, El Paso's Castner Range, Nevada's Gold Butte, and expand protections in lands surrounding Grand Canyon National Park."_
|
| [SIGN THE PETITION](http://democrats.ontraport.com/c/s/EFw/6jSZf/s/A/r5cs/6FTash/vc1Cebp3Fn) |
Activist - 2016 is the year for our public lands!

The National Park Service just turned 100, and shortly after President Obama made conservation history by creating the largest marine protected area in the world!

But it's not all good news. Pro-polluter members of Congress are trying to undermine the laws that protect our lands, while the Koch brothers and their polluter pals fight tooth and nail to gain access to areas around the Grand Canyon for dangerous uranium mining.

**That 's why the League of Conservation Voters (LCV) is joining with progressives across the country to keep our public lands out of polluters' greedy fingers by protecting them as national monuments.**

[Don't stop now!! Build on President Obama's conservation legacy and urge him to designate these four new national monuments »](http://democrats.ontraport.com/c/s/EFw/6jSZf/v/A/r5cs/6FTash/vWIBSV2CPC)

Here are four unique American landscapes we're asking President Obama to protect:
* **Utah 's Bears Ears region**, which holds 1.9 million acres of rich Native American history, an estimated 100,000 archeological sites, unique wildlife, and breathtaking views, is constantly under threat from Big Polluters, looting, and even vandalism. [Tell President Obama to protect Bears Ears now »](http://democrats.ontraport.com/c/s/EFw/6jSZf/z/A/r5cs/6FTash/zvHry0pd5P)
* **Texas 's Castner Range** near El Paso, a former U.S. military training facility, is home to more than 30 cultural sites on 7,000 acres and is deeply connected to the region's 14,000-year-old indigenous, scientific, and environmental history. [Tell President Obama to protect Castner Range now »](http://democrats.ontraport.com/c/s/EFw/6jSZf/U/A/r5cs/6FTash/6AIVVONYbs)
* **The Gold Butte region** , a 350,000-acre desertscape and Nevada's "piece of the Grand Canyon," located where the Great Basin, the Mojave and Sonoran deserts and Colorado Plateau all meet, but endangered by illegal water developments and vandalism. [Tell President Obama to protect Gold Butte now »](http://democrats.ontraport.com/c/s/EFw/6jSZf/j/A/r5cs/6FTash/6NBBfDQMAR)
* **The Greater Grand Canyon area** , 1.7 million acres ringing the canyon which houses the region's watershed and 3,000 ancient Native American archeological sites, but is being threatened by the development of nearby uranium mining pushed by Koch brothers-backed groups. [Tell President Obama to protect the Greater Grand Canyon now »](http://democrats.ontraport.com/c/s/EFw/6jSZf/5/A/r5cs/6FTash/vsxuVysHnC)

**[](http://democrats.ontraport.com/c/s/EFw/6jSZf/r/A/r5cs/6FTash/vQqPdQJSYE)President Obama has shown he is willing to act, protecting more land and water than any president in history.** Don't let these scenic regions be jeopardized by inappropriate development. Tell President Obama to create these four new national monuments so that future generations can enjoy our cultural and natural heritage.

[These places are under constant threat: Tell President Obama to act today »](http://democrats.ontraport.com/c/s/EFw/6jSZf/Y/A/r5cs/6FTash/zZrnvQDNri)

**We 've made so much progress, but there's still so much more to do to help us permanently protect these iconic places.** Sign the petition asking for the designation of these four areas as national monuments before the end of President Obama's administration.

Thank you,

Kristin Brown
Director of Digital Strategy
League of Conservation Voters

Every two years, Congress comes back after Election Day and spends their "Lame Duck" session trying to sneak harmful legislation through when they think Americans aren't paying attention. name: HillaryClinton.com
subject: Matt Lauer let Trump walk all over him
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Hillary for America, a grassroots campaign of 2
body:
we have to do what the press won't | Derek --

This week’s forum made something abundantly clear:

We cannot count on the media to hold Trump accountable all the time. **[We're going to have to do it ourselves -- chip in for the first time right now, get a sticker, and show that we have your support.](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=11&ms=MjY0NjUzNjYS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODgxNDY1OTc3S0&mt=1&rt=0)**

Matt Lauer let Donald Trump walk all over him on Wednesday. When Trump lied and said he'd opposed the Iraq War, Lauer smiled and moved on to the next question.

And you don't have to take my word for it. _The New York Times_ described that back and forth as "watching one student quiz another to prep for a test neither had done the reading for."

In pursuit of objectivity, some members of the press have lowered the bar for Trump, painting him as a run-of-the-mill candidate with strengths and weaknesses like anyone else -- and like Hillary.

But Trump is not typical, and this election is not normal. He is dangerous, purposefully divisive, and has a tenuous relationship with the truth.

So that means, in this campaign, we have to do what the press won't. We have to run ads and staff organizers in critical places where we can get the truth to voters to make sure no matter what headlines they read, they know the stakes.

**And that all takes resources. It takes your help, Derek. Will you chip in right now to pick up where the media has failed?** [](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=6&ms=MjY0NjUzNjYS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODgxNDY1OTc3S0&mt=1&rt=0)
Thanks,

Christina

body:
|

Friend,

I wanted to send you a quick update as we hit the 60 day out mark in this U.S. Senate election.

Yesterday our campaign learned that Chris' Republican opponent released a signaling memo with instructions for big money groups like Citizens United to attack Chris and try to change the course of this race.

With an opponent in the pocket of special interests and big donors abandoning Trump's presidential campaign to focus on down ballot races nationwide, we don't know exactly what to expect. One thing is clear -- our people-powered campaign needs to be ready for whatever comes our way.

**[Maryland can't afford another November surprise. We need to be prepared. Please donate $25 now to let Chris know you've got his back and to ensure we have the funds to get the job done. ](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91ae6/70cdc2ba/4713fc3a/39749a5/2858306938/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/cvh-keep-fighting?refcode=em090916_1&amount=25.00&recurring=12&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=1+-+Maryland+canrsquot+afford+another+Novemb&utm_campaign=em090916_1&source=em090916_1")**

After crisscrossing the state this summer, Chris is back in session this week working to make sure the Tea Party doesn't orchestrate another government shutdown, pushing Republicans to permit a vote on banning terrorists from purchasing guns, and providing desperately needed funding to combat the Zika virus - among other things.

**[He's standing up for us. Now is the time to stand up for him. Chip in now>> ](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91ae6/70cdc2ba/4713fc3a/39749a2/2858306938/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/cvh-keep-fighting?refcode=em090916_1&amount=25.00&recurring=12&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=2+-+Hersquos+standing+up+for+us+Now+is+the+t&utm_campaign=em090916_1&source=em090916_1")**

Thank you for standing with Chris.

Pat Murray
Campaign Manager
Van Hollen for Senate

| Greetings,
---
| We had a big week here on Team Scott as Phil introduced his comprehensive health care plan at a press event in Burlington and participated in a number of Labor Day festivities. Here are a few of this week’s highlights from the state’s most energetic and hardworking campaign.

**GETTING HEALTHCARE REFORM BACK ON TRACK**
Phil demonstrated his leadership in the effort to make healthcare more affordable for Vermonters by releasing his comprehensive healthcare plan this week. Detailing his approach for **getting healthcare reform back on track and increasing affordable choices for families and businesses** , Phil is the only candidate with a plan to address one of the most critical issues facing Vermonters and impacting our economy.

As Phil told the crowd at Wednesday’s event, **under one-party rule in Montpelier, Vermont has seen healthcare costs _increased by more than 20%_** **over the last six years.** You can view Phil’s full plan [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=eaf47c7555&e=19cebfe173). His top priorities include:
* Finally putting an **end to our failed experiment by responsibly transitioning from Vermont Health Connect** ;
* Fully **implementing and enforcing the law requiring price transparency** in our healthcare system;
* Lobbying the federal government to **amend the federal Affordable Care Act** to allow Vermonters to purchase insurance across state lines;
* Transitioning to a payment system that f**airly compensates providers for treatment that emphasizes quality and outcomes** , not just the amount of care they deliver and **eliminating the legal necessity for providers to practice “defensive medicine.”**

Photo credit: @PhilScott4VT

***
**DIRECT FROM PHIL: DISABILITY ISSUES AND SUPPORTIING OUR KIDS**
As Phil has made clear, the economy will be his number one priority. **Making Vermont more affordable will _attract and retain more families and businesses_ , strengthening our economy and _growing revenue organically_ , which allows us to _better serve and support all our citizens_ and communities**. Affordability and economic issues impact all Vermonters, and Phil took time this week to share how his policies and approach to governing will impact two important populations: our kids, and those citizens with disabilities.

Responding to questions posed by Let’s Grow Kids, Phil **stressed the importance of affordable child care and his commitment to making smart investments in early learning opportunities**. He outlined his multi-pronged approach to supporting families and expanding affordable child care options, and reiterated his support of the accountable implementation of Vermont’s universal pre-K law, Act 166. As Governor, he will closely follow its implementation, and work with the legislature to address any shortfalls. Read his full response [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=f12565b1a5&e=19cebfe173).

As the son of a veteran who lost both of his legs in World Wart II, disability issues are very important to Phil. Responding to RespectAbility, Phil shared more on **his record of supporting the disabled community –**[**particularly disabled veterans**](http://philscott.us13.list-manage2.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=7fd785fe62&e=19cebfe173)**– and his plans to further enable and empower disabled Vermonters** as Governor. View the full Q&A [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=fb7d433f17&e=19cebfe173).
******* **TWO MONTHS TO MAKE YOUR VOICE HEARD!**
Photo credit: @PhilScott4VT
A lot is at stake on November 8 – there is a very clear choice in this election. We cannot afford a fourth term of one-party rule, resulting in greater spending that’s paid for through more taxes and fees on Vermonters. But **we need your support over the next two months – and then we need your vote on November 8.** We’ve already seen that our opponents plan to run a negative campaign focused on attacks instead of putting forth their own solutions.

So what can you do to help? Here are a few ways to show your support and ensure we elect Phil Scott as the next Governor of Vermont:
* Stay informed on Phil’s [record](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=9f4194d4f6&e=19cebfe173), [priorities](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=4e5a83dea8&e=19cebfe173) and [policy proposals](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=07ea450507&e=19cebfe173) and share with friends and family. Visit [www.philscott.org](http://philscott.us13.list-manage2.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=39384eeb56&e=19cebfe173) to learn more. You can also learn more about our campaign and other Republicans through the Vermont GOP (http://vtgop.org).
* Follow us on [Facebook](http://philscott.us13.list-manage2.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=4147751f60&e=19cebfe173), [Twitter](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=327c8b90b3&e=19cebfe173) and [Instagram](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=94001e2204&e=19cebfe173) and share your support!
* Participate in our [events](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=ef26538f62&e=19cebfe173). This weekend we’ll be participating in the [Walk to End Alzheimer’s](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=c19d69b253&e=19cebfe173) and judging the [Vermont Farmstead Mac n’ Cheese Challenge](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=758626a7cd&e=19cebfe173). We’ll also be at the [Addison County Fall Dinner](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=08da763f92&e=19cebfe173).
* Better yet, don’t just attend an event - [volunteer](http://philscott.us13.list-manage2.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=13d0b78b53&e=19cebfe173). **We have opportunities for everyone, whether you can give a lot of time or just a little**. Our volunteers help by knocking on doors, making phone calls, staffing or hosting events and fundraisers, or even by just displaying a lawn sign or writing a Letter to the Editor.
* [DONATE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=6c0ec7556a&e=19cebfe173) to the campaign. This will continue to be a **tough campaign** and we will need resources to ensure Vermonters know the truth about where Phil stands on the issues and how hard he’ll fight for Vermonters. Every dollar helps – **let’s demonstrate just how much _grassroots support from Vermont_ Phil has**!

***
**ICYMI: PHIL IN THE NEWS**

**_Phil’s healthcare plan just what small businesses need_**
“Betsy Bishop, executive director of the Vermont Chamber of Commerce, says Scott’s plan offers the kind of fresh approach that small businesses have been pining for.” (Source: Vermont Public Radio: [Full Story](http://philscott.us13.list-manage2.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=16b1e95c39&e=19cebfe173))

**_Phil on Vermont’s outrageous healthcare costs_**
"The costs are increasing faster than Vermont’s ability to pay for it. What benefit are we providing people if we require them to pay for healthcare that they cannot afford?” (Source: VT Digger: [Full Story](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=e354e71636&e=19cebfe173))

**_Phil: Healthcare a driving force in affordability crisis_**
“For a lot of people who live here or who are thinking about living here, I think the affordability crisis will be the defining issue during this election. One of the many reasons Vermont is unaffordable for so many Vermonters is because of the cost of health care.” (Source: Times Argus: [Full Story](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=dfa09e537f&e=19cebfe173))

**_Save the Ridgelines: Support Phil Scott_**
“With the wiser policies advocated by Phil Scott we can reduce our carbon footprint, protect our landscape and reduce our energy costs.” Dave Kelley, Greensboro (Source: Montpelier Bridge: [Full Letter](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=fdb570f7c6&e=19cebfe173))
***
**ICYMI: THIS WEEK’S #GOODREADS**

**_Stand Opposed to Carbon Tax_**
Phil Scott knows too many Vermonters are struggling under the burden of Vermont’s crisis of affordability and he firmly believes we cannot raise taxes. He has definitively stated he will not support any new transportation taxes or the carbon tax. St. Johnsbury House district Reps. Scott Beck and Janssen Willhoit highlighted the impact of a Carbon Tax on Vermonters. They write in the _Caledonian Record_ , “Vermont’s carbon tax can be summed up simply: replace a regressive tax with another regressive tax that will have the added effect of damaging manufacturing and agriculture.” You can read the full Letter [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=19d0b9d51d&e=19cebfe173), and learn more about Phil’s transportation priorities [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=8a0ed381e7&e=19cebfe173).
*******
Thanks to everyone for continuing to follow and support our campaign. Be sure to follow along with the campaign’s activities every day – and share your thoughts – [@PhilScott4VT](http://philscott.us13.list-manage2.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=ff0a04cfaf&e=19cebfe173) and [https://www.facebook.com/PhilScottforVermont/](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=ce67cf0a77&e=19cebfe173).

Sincerely,
Team Scott

Every dollar helps – let’s demonstrate just how much grassroots support from Vermont Phil has! * ICYMI: PHIL IN THE NEWS Phil’s healthcare plan just what small businesses need “Betsy Bishop, executive director of the Vermont Chamber of Commerce, says Scott’s plan offers the kind of fresh approach that small businesses have been pining for.” (Source: Vermont Public Radio: ) Phil on Vermont’s outrageous healthcare costs "The costs are increasing faster than Vermont’s ability to pay for it. What benefit are we providing people if we require them to pay for healthcare that they cannot afford?” (Source: VT Digger: ) Phil: Healthcare a driving force in affordability crisis “For a lot of people who live here or who are thinking about living here, I think the affordability crisis will be the defining issue during this election. One of the many reasons Vermont is unaffordable for so many Vermonters is because of the cost of health care.” (Source: Times Argus: ) Save the Ridgelines: Support Phil Scott “With the wiser policies advocated by Phil Scott we can reduce our carbon footprint, protect our landscape and reduce our energy costs.” Dave Kelley, Greensboro (Source: Montpelier Bridge: ) ICYMI: THIS WEEK’S #GOODREADS Stand Opposed to Carbon Tax Phil Scott knows too many Vermonters are struggling under the burden of Vermont’s crisis of affordability and he firmly believes we cannot raise taxes. He has definitively stated he will not support any new transportation taxes or the carbon tax. St. Johnsbury House district Reps. Scott Beck and Janssen Willhoit highlighted the impact of a Carbon Tax on Vermonters. They write in the Caledonian Record , “Vermont’s carbon tax can be summed up simply: replace a regressive tax with another regressive tax that will have the added effect of damaging manufacturing and agriculture.” You can read the full Letter , and learn more about Phil’s transportation priorities . Thanks to everyone for continuing to follow and support our campaign. Be sure to follow along with the campaign’s activities every day – and share your thoughts – and . subject: update:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Van Hollen for US Senate
body:
| Friend, our opponent has released a signaling memo instructing outside groups to run 'independent' attack ads against us.

Chris has always run people-powered campaigns - he needs your support now. [**Chip in $25 or whatever you can to help us fund our response.**](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91ae4/70cdc2ba/47126ff4/39748d2/929412334/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/cvh-keep-fighting?refcode=em090916_2&amount=25.00&recurring=12&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=1+-+Chip+in+25+or+whatever+you+can+to+help+u&utm_campaign=em090916_2&source=em090916_2")

_Begin Forwarded Message:_

> Friend,
>
> I wanted to send you a quick update as we hit the 60 day out mark in this U.S. Senate election.
>
> Yesterday our campaign learned that Chris' Republican opponent released a signaling memo with instructions for big money groups like Citizens United to attack Chris and try to change the course of this race.
>
> With an opponent in the pocket of special interests and big donors abandoning Trump's presidential campaign to focus on down ballot races nationwide, we don't know exactly what to expect. One thing is clear -- our people-powered campaign needs to be ready for whatever comes our way.
>
> **[Maryland can't afford another November surprise. We need to be prepared. Please donate $25 now to let Chris know you've got his back and to ensure we have the funds to get the job done. ](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91ae4/70cdc2ba/47126ff4/39748d3/929412334/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/cvh-keep-fighting?refcode=em090916_2&amount=25.00&recurring=12&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=2+-+Maryland+canrsquot+afford+another+Novemb&utm_campaign=em090916_2&source=em090916_2")**
>
> After crisscrossing the state this summer, Chris is back in session this week working to make sure the Tea Party doesn't orchestrate another government shutdown, pushing Republicans to permit a vote on banning terrorists from purchasing guns, and providing desperately needed funding to combat the Zika virus - among other things.
>
> **[He's standing up for us. Now is the time to stand up for him. Chip in now>> ](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91ae4/70cdc2ba/47126ff4/39748d0/929412334/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/cvh-keep-fighting?refcode=em090916_2&amount=25.00&recurring=12&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=3+-+Hersquos+standing+up+for+us+Now+is+the+t&utm_campaign=em090916_2&source=em090916_2")**
>
> Thank you for standing with Chris.
>
> Pat Murray
> Campaign Manager
> Van Hollen for Senate

When Eric decided to run for office he knew it would be tough. But he never imagined that he would get attacked for helping veterans.
